metadata.title:
NEARLY BILLION
GIVEN AWAY


BY CITY

pages.raw_text:
The advice OF the water commits
sion that city water given away
heretofore be paid for out of the
general fund and credited to the
water department brings to light
some interesting facts in regard to
the amount of water that is dis
tributed freely or sold at an incon-
sequential rate in this city Secre-
tary Ostrander of the commission
estimates that the amount will
reach 20 per cent. This leaves the
total cost of pumping upon the 80
per cent actually paid by con-
sumers. The plan of charging the
20 per cent to the general fund is
designed to place the cost of the
water plant upon the public in
general instead of the 80 per cent
water consumers.


The water included on the free
list is that furnished schools. parks
and charitable institutions. Here
are some of those to whom water
has been given by the council, with
the amount in gallons:


Ondawa inn, 1864, $50; Home of
the Friendless. 100000; st. Joseph
orphanage, 100000.


The biggest item is street sprin-
king by the street car companies.
The Washington Water Power Co.
uses 19,764,000 and the Traction
Co. 100000, although you would
never suspect 1t of either from the
condition of their roadbeds. The
total income to the water depart
ment from this source would be
$3465 daily If the water "a were
charged for.


In addition to the free list, the
S. A. A. c. and country club are
furnished water at the charity rate
of four cents per 1000 gallons; the
S. A. A. Z. uses practically 100000
gallons a year Sacred Heart hos
pital secures 2,S34,000 gallons a
year at So per month; st. Luke's,
100000 at the same rate.


In addition to this are the city
street sprinklers and fire plugs and
numerous other sources of use of
city without credit to the city for
furnishing it.


The total of the free and reduced
water list for last year was 72S,-
210,610 gallons. Credit that to the
water department at seven cents
per 1000 gallons and any doubt as
to the profit of the department
would vanish.
